summary: Filled the contracted M7 Beta scenario/person/event content path through explicit source schema, compiler, runtime pack, tests, and a reviewable content-source JSON. The fill records 3 composite scenario candidates, 6 composite person role records, 3 language-review-blocked title records, 5 event hooks, 36 localization records, 6 claim records, source labels, owners, review states, deterministic ordering, and explicit known gaps. It does not create formal content lock acceptance, formal page-verified real-world claims, sim rules, UI behavior, save migration, release artifacts, telemetry, networking, server behavior, arbitrary-code mods, or manual node battle work.

tests_run.result: Before review commit: allowed task changes present plus unrelated forbidden untracked docs/M7_UI_Localization_Art_Handoff_Prompts.md left untouched.
Task list shows M7-SCENARIO-PERSON-EVENT-FILL-001 in REVIEW.
M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001 is the only ready output while this task is in REVIEW.

tests_run.result: shape ok: task REVIEW; M7 beta set counts scenarios=3 persons=6 titles=3 events=5 localization=36 claims=6; content lock not accepted; DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and PASS_WITH_LIMITS preserved.
Content validation passed for M1 hash 4a438525, M2 hash 9ab72b0b, M3 hash 76b75849, M6 map hash 4485a353, M6 scenario hash 6d1f6d64, and M7 beta scenario/person/event set hash 92aed91e.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed; architecture self-test produced 15 expected violations.

decisions: Added an M7-specific scenario/person/event source schema instead of serializing runtime object graphs or extending sim-core.
Kept all scenario anchors as COMPOSITE/RESEARCH REQUIRED candidates; no Beta real-world scenario, person, title, or chronology claim is marked as formal historical acceptance.
Used composite role records for persons and language-review-blocked generic title records rather than inventing real names, titles, transliterations, or genealogies.

Included a high-risk coercion/forced-movement event hook only with explicit victim groups, source-region RESEARCH REQUIRED text, immediate costs, long-term consequences, and CULTURE_HUMAN_GATE_REQUIRED state.
Connected M7 content validation to pnpm content:validate so the new JSON is compiled with the existing content path.
Preserved M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS and DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly.
risks: docs/09-* was requested by standing historical instructions but no docs/09 file exists in this checkout; I read docs/10-historical-research-policy.md, docs/16-research-bibliography.md, docs/19-data-schema-guide.md, the accepted M7 review baseline, and recorded the missing docs/09 as a reading discrepancy rather than inventing it.
Academic sources in the M7 fill are candidate/bibliographic source records only; I did not claim full-text page verification for Baker/Pasuk 2017 or Charney 2004.

Formal names, titles, aliases, transliterations, exact historical participants, chronology, symbols, art, music, clothing, architecture, and religious imagery remain RESEARCH REQUIRED or Human Gate material.
Formal content lock acceptance remains M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 and is not created here.
requested_action: Review the M7 scenario/person/event fill path for schema discipline, source labels, owner/review-state coverage, deterministic ordering, explicit known gaps, violence-cost handling, no modern identity shortcut, no unsupported historical certainty, no formal content lock acceptance, no manual node battle work, and compatibility with accepted M1-M6 content/runtime boundaries.
